rule: Camera must remain on throughout the entire session. Turning off the camera is a flaggable offense ONLY if the tutor deliberately turns it off for an extended period (more than 1 minute) without a valid reason. If the camera is turned off BRIEFLY (under 1 minute) due to internet/connectivity issues — either tutor-side or student-side — this is NORMAL and should NOT be flagged.
flag_level: Red if repeated, Yellow if first-time (only if deliberate and >1 minute)
standard_comment: S - Camera Quality: Kindly be informed that it is mandatory to keep the webcam activated for the entire session.
scoring_note: Do NOT flag if camera was briefly turned off due to internet issues (tutor or student side). Brief camera-off (<1 min) to stabilize connection is NORMAL behavior. Only flag if the tutor deliberately keeps the camera off for extended periods without a valid reason.

rule: Camera angle should be at eye level for clear engagement. Low or side angles reduce connection. CRITICAL: Only flag the TUTOR's camera angle. If the tutor is INSTRUCTING the student to adjust their camera (e.g. Arabic: 'ممكن نزل الكاميرا شوية' = 'Can you lower the camera a bit'), this is POSITIVE tutor behavior (coaching the student), NOT a tutor setup issue.
standard_comment: S - Camera Quality: The camera angle was not properly set. Kindly adjust the camera to eye level for clearer communication.
scoring_note: CRITICAL: Before flagging camera angle, determine WHO the comment is about. If the tutor says 'ممكن نزل/ارفع الكاميرا' (can you lower/raise the camera), they are instructing the STUDENT — this is POSITIVE behavior, not a flag. Only flag if the TUTOR's OWN camera is at a bad angle.

rule: Camera image quality should be clear enough for expressions and gestures to be visible.
standard_comment: S - Camera Quality: The camera image quality appeared noticeably low, making it difficult for the student to clearly see expressions and gestures.

rule: Microphone should be clear and audible. Low volume, static, or harsh audio affects learning.
standard_comment: S - Microphone Quality: The volume was occasionally unclear, which may have made it hard for the student to hear.

rule: Microphone should be muted before student joins the session.
standard_comment: S - Microphone Quality: Kindly ensure that your microphone is muted until the student joins the session.

rule: Must use the official iSchool Zoom virtual background with the correct logo. Mirrored logos should be fixed in Zoom settings.
standard_comment: S - Environment: Your professionalism in using the iSchool logo as your backdrop is greatly appreciated. Just ensure it is not mirrored by adjusting your Zoom settings.

rule: Sessions must not be conducted in public places. A quiet, private space is required.
standard_comment: S - Environment: Background noise or disturbances were present. Please ensure a quiet environment suitable for learning.

rule: Lighting should be adequate - not too dim, not too bright. Face must be clearly visible.
standard_comment: S - Environment: The lighting during the session could be improved. A well-lit environment with balanced lighting improves visual clarity and engagement.

rule: No distracting objects (cups, phones, etc.) in front of the camera. No eating or chewing gum.
standard_comment: S - Environment: Kindly avoid placing distracting objects in front of the camera during the session.

rule: Internet connection should be stable. ONLY flag tutor-side internet issues that cause disconnection for MORE THAN 1 MINUTE. Brief lags, momentary freezes, or short disconnections (under 1 minute) are NORMAL in online sessions and should NOT be flagged. CRITICAL: Distinguish TUTOR internet issues from STUDENT internet issues — if the STUDENT has internet problems (student's camera freezes, student disconnects, student asks to turn off camera due to their internet), do NOT penalize the tutor.
flag_level: Yellow ONLY if tutor-side disconnection >1 minute, repeated 3+ times
standard_comment: S - Internet Quality: Occasional internet lags disrupted the session flow and made it harder for the student to stay engaged.
scoring_note: CRITICAL: Only flag TUTOR-SIDE internet issues lasting MORE THAN 1 MINUTE. Student-side internet issues (student disconnects, student camera freezes, student asks to close camera) are NOT the tutor's fault and must NOT be flagged against the tutor. Brief lags (<1 min) are normal in online sessions. Arabic clues: if someone says 'النت عندك بيقطع' (your internet is cutting) TO the student, that means STUDENT has the problem, not the tutor.

rule: Semi-formal or formal attire required. Sportswear, pajamas, or inappropriate clothing is flaggable.
flag_level: Yellow first time, Red if repeated
standard_comment: S - Dress Code: Your attention is kindly requested to strictly adhere to the formal or semi-formal dress attire policy.
scoring_note: Only flag if clearly inappropriate (sportswear, pajamas). Normal casual wear is acceptable. Do NOT flag if unclear from video.

rule: Tutor should maintain a professional, upright posture. Relaxed leaning or resting head on hand is distracting.
standard_comment: S - Environment: Maintaining a more formal and upright posture helps support a focused and professional learning environment.

rule: Tutor should maintain eye contact with camera. Looking between two screens or downward limits engagement.
standard_comment: S - Environment: For better engagement, keep the primary focus toward the front-facing screen or camera.

rule: Session should begin with light, friendly questions to build rapport. Ice-breakers recommended for first sessions.
standard_comment: A - Session Initiation & Closure: Beginning the session with light, friendly questions can support a more engaging and student-centered atmosphere.

rule: Session should end with a warm goodbye and thank-you. Abrupt endings feel disrespectful.
standard_comment: A - Session Initiation & Closure: Ending the session with a warm goodbye helps leave a positive impression.

rule: Tutor should smile and engage warmly. Monotone or harsh delivery affects learning.
standard_comment: A - Friendliness: Consider smiling and engaging in friendly conversation to build rapport and enhance message delivery.

rule: Shouting or intimidating behavior is flaggable. Harsh tone with struggling students is unacceptable.
flag_level: Red if severe, Yellow if mild
standard_comment: A - Friendliness: Please maintain a calm and supportive tone to foster a positive learning environment.

rule: Professional, age-appropriate language required. Informal/casual expressions affect professional tone.
standard_comment: A - Language Used: Maintaining more formal language helps create a focused and respectful learning environment.

rule: Political or religious topics must be avoided. Sessions should remain neutral and inclusive.
standard_comment: A - Language Used: Political or religious topics should be avoided to maintain a neutral and inclusive learning environment.

rule: Tone should be active and lively, not monotonous. Signs of drowsiness or fatigue are unacceptable.
standard_comment: A - Voice Tone & Clarity: It is vital not to convey signs of drowsiness or fatigue, as it could distract the student.

rule: Technical terms should be pronounced correctly. Mispronunciation affects credibility.
standard_comment: A - Language Used: Before the session, please ensure that any technical words you plan to use are pronounced correctly.

rule: Avoid blaming students for incomplete homework. Use supportive, encouraging language.
standard_comment: A - Friendliness: While addressing incomplete homework, kindly avoid blaming or speaking harshly. Guide with encouragement.

rule: Mentioning iSchool brand name at the start and key moments builds brand loyalty.
standard_comment: [Advice] - Mentioning iSchool at the outset and conclusion cultivates brand loyalty.

rule: Sessions should be conducted in English. Casual phrases or connectors in Arabic should be minimized.
standard_comment: A - Language Used: Kindly ensure full consistency in using English throughout the session.

rule: Presentation must be displayed in full-screen/slide show mode. No browser tab distractions.
standard_comment: P - Project software & slides: When sharing the presentation, kindly ensure it is displayed in full-screen mode to avoid distractions.

rule: All tools and software should be opened and ready before the session starts.
standard_comment: P - Project software & slides: Preparing all tools beforehand can save time and help students stay focused.

rule: Use desktop versions of tools (Godot, PictoBlox, ScratchJr) for stability.
standard_comment: P - Project software & slides: Kindly adhere to using the desktop version for better performance and stability.

rule: Tutor should review lesson content thoroughly. Hesitation or lack of confidence indicates poor preparation.
standard_comment: P - Session study: While generally prepared, the explanation lacked confidence at times. A quick review could help improve delivery.

rule: Incorrect information in response to student questions is a flaggable offense.
standard_comment: P - Knowledge About Subject: Incorrect information was provided. Please review lesson content thoroughly before sessions.

rule: Project code should be tested and robust before the session. Bugs during implementation indicate poor preparation.
standard_comment: P - Session study: Given challenges with bugs during implementation, more thorough preparation would ensure smoother sessions.

rule: Internal platforms (LMS, Dashboard, Slack) should not be displayed to students.
standard_comment: P - Project software & slides: Internal platforms were displayed. Please avoid showing internal tools during sessions.

rule: Personal contact information (email, WhatsApp, phone number) must not be visible during session.
standard_comment: P - Project software & slides: Kindly ensure that personal contact information is not visible to maintain data confidentiality.

rule: Q&A answers should be reviewed beforehand. Incorrect answers during Q&A are flagged.
standard_comment: P - Session study: Please ensure Q&A slides are reviewed before starting to ensure accuracy.

rule: All slides must be covered. Skipping slides (especially Q&A and presentation slides) is a deduction.
standard_comment: C - Slides and project completion: Ensure effective time management to avoid omission of slides.

rule: Project must be completed during the session. Incomplete projects due to poor time management are noted.
standard_comment: C - Slides and project completion: The project was not fully completed. Better time allocation could help ensure full hands-on practice.

rule: Students must NOT take screenshots of slides. All material is on the student dashboard.
standard_comment: C - Slides and project completion: It is not allowed for students to take screenshots of the slides.

rule: Zoom annotation tools should be used instead of mouse pointer for explaining concepts.
standard_comment: C - Tools used and Methodology: Using Zoom annotation tools instead of the mouse pointer would provide clearer guidance.

rule: Only pre-approved materials and tools should be used. Unapproved external resources are flaggable.
standard_comment: C - Tools used and Methodology: Please only use pre-approved materials to stay aligned with the curriculum.

rule: Videos must be shared with audio enabled.
standard_comment: C - Tools used and Methodology: Kindly ensure audio is shared when playing videos.

rule: Homework must be properly reviewed, not just asked about. Walk through incomplete homework with student.
standard_comment: C - Homework: It is not enough to just check if homework was done. Please walk through a quick overview.

rule: Students must receive clear homework instructions before session ends.
standard_comment: C - Homework: Kindly ensure students receive clear instructions about upcoming homework before the session ends.

rule: Teacher Guide (TG) must be strictly followed. Deviating from the TG is noted.
standard_comment: C - Slides and project completion: The project was not fully completed as the teacher guide was not followed.

rule: Student must lead the presentation segment. Tutor should not take over.
standard_comment: C - Slides and project completion: The student is expected to lead the presentation segment.

rule: Videos should be paused at key points to discuss content with the student.
standard_comment: C - Tools used and Methodology: It would be beneficial to pause the video to discuss key points.

rule: Avoid going too deep into topics not age-appropriate or that interrupt session flow.
standard_comment: C - Tools used and Methodology: Please avoid going too deep into a topic not suitable for the student age.

rule: Zoom meeting controls must not overlap with the presentation.
standard_comment: C - Tools used and Methodology: Kindly ensure that the Zoom meeting controls do not overlap with the presentation.

rule: Avoid long stretches of one-directional explaining. Ask open-ended questions and involve the student.
standard_comment: T - Student Engagement: Kindly avoid spending long stretches explaining without involving the student.

rule: During checkup, engage the student with participation rather than re-explaining concepts.
standard_comment: T - Student Engagement: Kindly engage the student during the checkup by encouraging participation.

rule: Tutor should not remain silent during student implementation. Active engagement is required.
standard_comment: T - Student Engagement: Kindly enhance class interactivity during project implementation.

rule: Student must independently implement the project. Step-by-step hand-holding should be limited.
standard_comment: T - Project Implementation & Activities: Encourage the student to rely more on their own reasoning.

rule: AVOID endorsing direct code copying. Students should implement independently.
standard_comment: T - Project Implementation & Activities: Kindly avoid endorsing direct code copying. Encourage independent learning.

rule: Divide project into smaller sections. Demonstrate first, then let student try.
standard_comment: T - Project Implementation & Activities: It may be helpful to divide the project into smaller sections followed by student implementation.

rule: Student must share screen during project implementation.
standard_comment: T - Project Implementation & Activities: The student should share their screen during project implementation.

rule: Students must upload completed projects to the dashboard. Tutor must assist.
standard_comment: T - Project Implementation & Activities: Students must upload their completed projects to the dashboard.

rule: Project description must be detailed and include code images.
standard_comment: T - Project Implementation & Activities: Please ensure project descriptions are detailed and all criteria included.

rule: Tutor must NOT take control of student screen via remote control to complete their project.
standard_comment: T - Project Implementation & Activities: Guide the student instead of directly completing tasks for them.

rule: Session duration must be strictly 1 hour (+-10 minutes).
standard_comment: T - Class Management: The session duration should be strictly adhered to (1 hour +-10 minutes).

rule: Tutor must join 10 minutes before session start time.
standard_comment: T - Class Management: Kindly ensure your presence 10 minutes ahead of the session.

rule: No personal phone calls or texts during session.
standard_comment: T - Class Management: Please avoid using your phone for personal matters while teaching.

rule: Breaks should not exceed 5 minutes.
standard_comment: T - Class Management: Breaks should not exceed 5 minutes.

rule: All session components (Q&A, recap, attendance, presentation) must be covered in order.
standard_comment: T - Session Synchronization: Key components were skipped. Ensure all essential elements are included.

rule: Follow the planned session sequence. Timeline deviations should be minimized.
standard_comment: T - Session Synchronization: Occasional timeline deviations were observed.

rule: Internal Feedback must include details about any issues during the session.
standard_comment: T - Class Management: Kindly provide more details in the Internal Feedback regarding any issue.

rule: No direct discussions with parents. Refer to the designated agent.
standard_comment: T - Class Management: Please refrain from direct discussions with parents.

rule: Session content must not be changed or a different lesson taught.
standard_comment: T - Class Management: Changing the content or teaching a different lesson is strictly not allowed.

rule: Tutor must not criticize or make negative remarks about session content to students.
standard_comment: T - Class Management: Kindly refrain from making negative remarks about session content.

rule: During presentation segment, allow students to present what they have learned.
standard_comment: T - Student Engagement: During the presentation segment, allow students to share what they have learned.

rule: Pause videos to discuss content with the student rather than playing continuously.
standard_comment: T - Student Engagement: It would be beneficial to pause the video to discuss it with the student.

rule: Ensure students maintain professional conduct (no inappropriate language, proper seating).
standard_comment: T - Class Management: Ensure students maintain professional conduct during the session.

rule: Feedback must vary between sessions for the same student.
standard_comment: F - Feedback Quality: Kindly vary the feedback provided across sessions.

rule: Feedback must align with what actually happened in the session. No generic or unrelated comments.
standard_comment: F - Feedback Quality: The feedback could be more aligned with what occurred during the session.

rule: Feedback section must not be left empty.
standard_comment: F - Feedback Quality: Kindly avoid leaving the feedback section empty.

rule: Written feedback must align with the star rating.
standard_comment: F - Feedback Quality: Please ensure that the written comment aligns with the given star rating.

rule: Avoid using the same feedback across multiple students.
standard_comment: F - Feedback Quality: Please avoid using the same feedback across multiple students.

rule: Feedback should include both positive reinforcement AND suggestions for improvement.
standard_comment: F - Feedback Quality: Kindly ensure that feedback includes both positive reinforcement and suggestions for improvement.

rule: Feedback should be submitted promptly after the session.
standard_comment: F - Feedback Quality: Please share feedback promptly after each session.

rule: All student feedback must be written in English.
standard_comment: F - Feedback Quality: Kindly ensure that all student feedback is written in English.

rule: Any rating below 5 stars must include a reason.
standard_comment: F - Feedback Quality: Kindly include reasons for any ratings below 5.

rule: SCORING CALIBRATION: Use these anchors when determining scores.

scoring_guide.95-100: Outstanding - zero or one minor cosmetic issue (e.g., logo mirrored). This is the EXPECTED range for most sessions.
scoring_guide.88-94: Good - two to three low-severity issues with supporting evidence.
scoring_guide.80-87: Acceptable - moderate issues found (e.g., camera off segment, one Q&A answer incorrect).
scoring_guide.70-79: Needs Improvement - multiple moderate or one high-severity issue found.
scoring_guide.Below 70: Serious Concerns - multiple high-severity issues (harsh tone + skipped content + no student implementation).

rule: The tutor should maintain eye contact with the camera during explanations. Minimal eye contact (looking away from camera, reading from another screen) makes the student feel disconnected.
standard_comment: A - Friendliness: There was minimal eye contact from the tutor, which may make the student feel disconnected. Expressive, friendly visuals can support comfort and trust.

rule: While addressing incomplete homework or mistakes, the tutor must remain encouraging and supportive. Blaming, speaking harshly, or showing frustration is not acceptable.
standard_comment: A - Friendliness: While it's important to address incomplete homework, kindly avoid blaming or speaking harshly to students. Instead, guide them with encouragement to help them stay motivated and engaged.

rule: The session must start with a warm welcome and ice-breaker. An abrupt start without greeting or warm-up makes younger students anxious and reduces rapport.
standard_comment: A - Session Initiation & Closure: The session started abruptly without any warm-up or welcome, which might make younger students feel anxious. Starting with a friendly question or quick chat helps ease them in.

rule: The tutor must not dictate code line-by-line to the student. Instead, the tutor should explain the concept and let the student write the code independently, providing hints when stuck.
standard_comment: T - Project Implementation & Activities: Extensive step-by-step guidance was provided during project implementation. Kindly offer limited support and encourage the student to think critically and solve problems independently.

rule: The project must be fully implemented during the session as per the teacher guide. Assigning the project as homework instead of completing it during the session is not acceptable.
standard_comment: T - Project Implementation & Activities: The project was not fully completed during the session as intended. Kindly adhere to the project content as outlined in the presentation slides.

rule: Session duration must be strictly 60 minutes (+/- 10 minutes). Sessions ending more than 10 minutes early (under 50 minutes) must be flagged. If ending early, use remaining time for extra activities.
standard_comment: T - Class Management: The session duration should be strictly adhered to (1 hour +-10 minutes). If the session ends early, please utilize the remaining time by providing extra programming activities.

rule: When showing a video during the session, the tutor should pause the video at key moments to discuss with the student and verify understanding.
standard_comment: C - Tools used and Methodology: It would be beneficial to pause the video at key moments to discuss it with the student and verify their understanding.

rule: When sharing screen to play a video, the tutor must ensure 'Share Sound' is checked in Zoom so the student can hear the video audio.
standard_comment: C - Tools used and Methodology: Kindly ensure audio is shared when playing videos to support student understanding and engagement.

rule: Students must upload their completed projects to the dashboard during the session. The tutor must guide and assist them through this process.
standard_comment: T - Project Implementation & Activities: Please be advised that students must upload their completed projects to the dashboard during the session. Your support in guiding them through this process is crucial.

rule: Personal or internal platforms (Discord, social media, personal chats) must not be visible to students during screen sharing to maintain data confidentiality.
standard_comment: S - Environment: Kindly ensure that personal or internal platforms are not visible to students during screen sharing in order to maintain data confidentiality and uphold professional boundaries.

rule: The tutor should not rush through recap or explanation slides. A slower, more thoughtful pace helps students engage actively and understand better.
standard_comment: C - Slides and project completion: Kindly avoid rushing through the recap slides. A slower and more thoughtful pace helps students engage actively and understand better.

rule: The tutor must avoid long stretches of explaining without involving the student. Open-ended questions and encouraging the student to reflect on explanations improve engagement.
standard_comment: T - Student Engagement: Kindly avoid spending long stretches explaining without involving the student. The session would benefit from asking more open-ended questions or encouraging the student to reflect.
